analytics.title: Telegram Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Analyze channel activity for the last 1 to 7 days.

fraud.title: Anti-Fraud
fraud.hint: Risk signals based on suspicious engagement patterns.

help.label: What is this data?
help.overview: Executive-friendly Telegram analytics with trends, engagement signals, and anti-fraud context for rapid decision-making.
help.formula: Post score formula. Variables: V = views, F = forwards, R = replies, Re = reactions, G = gifts. Calculation: score = V*wV + F*wF + R*wR + Re*wRe + G*wG.
help.antiFraud: Anti-Fraud score (0-100) aggregates suspicious engagement patterns and trigger clusters to prioritize review workload.
help.funnel: Shows the step-by-step conversion from all posts to posts with meaningful engagement.
help.audience: Shows author structure: concentration, recurring contributors, and peak activity hours.
help.activity: Shows period dynamics by bucket: number of posts, views, and interactions.
help.distribution: Shows which media formats and reactions dominate in the selected period.
help.opinionLeaders: Shows top authors by impact score and engagement, with trends by day.
help.topPosts: Shows the highest-impact posts ranked by weighted score.

empty.title: Pick a channel and period
empty.description: The analytics module aggregates live Telegram messages and renders charts, totals, and top posts for the selected period.

errors.rangeTooLong: The custom date range cannot exceed 7 days.

charts.activityHint: Messages, views, and interactions across the chosen range.
charts.funnelHint: Message flow from publication to engagement signals.
charts.audienceHint: Author activity and concentration for the selected period.
charts.distributionHint: Top media types and reaction groups.
charts.opinionLeadersHint: Top authors by impact, based on score and interactions.
charts.opinionLeadersByDayHint: Daily activity of opinion leaders within the selected range.
charts.author: Author
charts.topPostsHint: Posts ranked by an engagement score.

analytics.noTags: No tags found in public data.

fields.mode: Analysis mode: single video or channel.
fields.videoId: YouTube video ID or video URL. Used for single-video analytics.
fields.channelId: Channel ID, @handle, or username. Used to build analytics for recent channel publications.
fields.period: Quick period presets: last 1, 3, or 7 days.

fields.dateTo: End date for a custom period. Maximum range is 7 days.

help.insights: Quick interpretation of the current dataset: focus video, top video by views, engagement, and dominant duration.
help.timeline: Interactive day-by-day dynamics: hover to see exact values for views, likes, and comments.
help.distribution: Content mix by duration, quality, and captions. Bars show relative share inside the current dataset.
help.leaders: Leaderboards are ranked by the selected metric: views, likes, comments, and engagement.

metricHints.videos: Number of videos included in the analyzed dataset.
metricHints.views: Total views across all videos in the dataset.
metricHints.likes: Total likes across all videos in the dataset.
metricHints.comments: Total comments across all videos in the dataset.
metricHints.avgViews: Average views per video in the dataset.
metricHints.engagement: Engagement Rate: (likes + comments) divided by views, in percent.
metricHints.subscribers: Current channel subscriber count (if available in the public API).
metricHints.channelViews: Total channel views from the public API.
metricHints.medianViews: Median views: the middle value, less sensitive to outliers.

siteHealth.title: Site Health
siteHealth.description: Production-style health check for public web assets: DNS, SSL, redirects, status, and baseline hardening controls.
siteHealth.empty: Run a check to view site health details.

help.overview: Quickly assess external site trust and resilience using technical signals that matter for risk triage and monitoring.
help.dns: A/AAAA records show whether the domain resolves for IPv4 and IPv6.
help.ssl: Certificate details and validity period for HTTPS endpoint.
help.securityHeaders: Checks baseline hardening headers that reduce browser-side attack surface.
help.httpChain: Every request step before reaching final URL, including redirects and response time.

signal.http_error_status: Final HTTP status is an error

errors.checkFailed: Unable to complete analysis. Please retry in a moment.

domainLite.title: Domain Lite
domainLite.description: Concise domain intelligence baseline: WHOIS and DNS posture with practical risk scoring for fast triage.
domainLite.empty: Run lookup to view domain summary.
domainLite.riskScoreHint: 0-29 low risk, 30-59 medium, 60-100 high. Higher score means higher risk.
domainLite.noRiskBreakdown: No negative signals contributed to the score.

help.overview: Use Domain Lite to validate ownership, lifecycle, and mail-security posture before deeper investigation.
help.dns: DNS block includes infrastructure and email records (A/AAAA/NS/MX/SPF/DMARC).
help.whois: WHOIS provides registration metadata: registrar and domain lifecycle dates.
help.risk: Risk score increases when DNS/WHOIS has weak or missing data.

signal.spf_too_many_includes: SPF has too many include mechanisms
signal.new_domain_30_days: Domain age is less than 30 days
signal.new_domain_90_days: Domain age is less than 90 days

errors.lookupFailed: Unable to complete analysis. Please retry in a moment.

analytics.title: Site Intel Analytics
analytics.description: Combined analytics for site health and domain risk in one snapshot.
analytics.empty: Run analytics to view combined summary.

help.overview: Combines Site Health and Domain Lite into one score, risk signals, and recommendations.
help.scoreBreakdown: Shows how the snapshot looks across core dimensions: overall, health, domain safety, and security header coverage.
help.signalBalance: Compares the count of risk and strength signals detected in this snapshot.
help.dnsProfile: Displays relative DNS record availability (A/AAAA/NS/MX/TXT/CAA) for quick infrastructure sanity checks.
help.httpLatency: Visualizes response time per HTTP chain step. Spikes may indicate redirect overhead or unstable endpoints.
help.snapshotMetrics: Compact operational metadata from this run: SSL horizon, WHOIS availability, domain age, and email-security readiness.
help.riskSignals: Potentially harmful conditions aggregated from Site Health and Domain Lite.
help.strengthSignals: Positive indicators that improve confidence in target stability and security posture.
help.recommendations: Prioritized actions derived from detected signals. Impact is an approximate score uplift estimate.

recommendation.improve_security_headers: Improve security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.).
recommendation.configure_email_security: Configure SPF and DMARC for domain email security.
recommendation.renew_domain_early: Renew domain early to avoid expiration risks.
recommendation.maintain_current_posture: Current posture looks healthy; keep it maintained.

risk.http_error_status: Final HTTP status is an error
risk.spf_too_many_includes: SPF has too many include mechanisms
risk.new_domain_30_days: Domain age is less than 30 days
risk.new_domain_90_days: Domain age is less than 90 days

strength.https_enforced: HTTPS is enforced on final URL
strength.ssl_valid: SSL certificate is valid and not expiring soon

seoAudit.title: SEO Audit
seoAudit.description: Checks indexability, meta tags, robots/sitemap, links and technical SEO signals.
seoAudit.empty: Run SEO audit to see technical issues and prioritized recommendations.

help.overview: Quick SEO snapshot of crawlability, indexability, metadata quality, performance and security.
help.meta: Checks core snippets for search results: title, description, canonical and robots directives.
help.indexability: Combines meta robots and X-Robots-Tag to estimate whether the page can be indexed.
help.performance: Approximate page weight and response timing from a single request, useful for quick triage.
help.crawlFiles: robots.txt and sitemap.xml availability helps search bots discover and crawl pages correctly.
help.recommendations: Ordered checklist of fixes by urgency to improve crawlability and SEO quality.
help.platformType: Use manual type when auditing large platforms. Auto works for most sites, but explicit type gives more stable scoring.
help.scoreProfile: Scoring profile controls weights and thresholds. Different site classes are evaluated by different SEO expectations.
help.technicalFlags: Quick checks for responsive viewport, pagination signals, and soft-404 patterns.
help.contentQuality: Content density and quality signals: word count, text-to-HTML ratio, and thin-content heuristics.
help.accessibility: SEO-related accessibility checks: missing alt attributes, heading order and basic semantic issues.
help.linkGraph: Internal/external outlinks and orphan-page risk based on discovered linking patterns.
help.international: Hreflang consistency, x-default presence and language cluster coverage across crawled pages.
help.crawlBudget: Bot-hit and status distribution signals from available logs. If logs are not connected, this section is limited.

signal.title_length_out_of_range: Title length is outside recommended range
signal.description_length_out_of_range: Description length is outside recommended range
signal.duplicate_titles: Duplicate titles detected across crawled pages
signal.missing_canonical: Some crawled pages have no canonical
signal.sitemap_non_200_urls: Sitemap contains URLs returning non-200 status
signal.pagination_signals_incomplete: Pagination rel prev/next signals are incomplete
signal.hreflang_missing_x_default: Missing x-default hreflang on translated pages
signal.crawl_budget_no_bot_hits: No bot hits detected in logs for the period

recommendation.enable_indexing: Allow indexing: remove noindex from meta robots / X-Robots-Tag.
recommendation.fix_h1_count: Keep exactly one H1 on the page.
recommendation.add_robots_txt: Add robots.txt with correct crawler directives.
recommendation.add_sitemap: Add sitemap.xml and declare it in robots.txt.
recommendation.remove_mixed_content: Remove mixed content: all resources should load over HTTPS.
recommendation.maintain_current_setup: No critical issues found. Maintain current optimization quality.
recommendation.deduplicate_titles: Deduplicate page titles across crawled pages.
recommendation.set_canonical_on_all_pages: Set canonical tag on every indexable page.
recommendation.fix_hreflang_self_reference: Add self-referencing hreflang for translated pages.
recommendation.fix_sitemap_non_200_urls: Remove or fix sitemap URLs returning non-200 responses.
recommendation.fix_soft_404: Return proper 404/410 status for not-found content pages.
recommendation.reduce_render_blocking: Reduce render-blocking CSS/JS and defer non-critical scripts.
recommendation.fix_pagination_rel_links: Add consistent rel prev/next signals for paginated pages.
recommendation.fix_empty_anchor_text: Replace empty/generic anchor texts with descriptive link text.
recommendation.add_image_alt_attributes: Add alt attributes to informative images.
recommendation.expand_thin_content: Expand thin content with useful topic-relevant text.
recommendation.improve_text_html_ratio: Reduce boilerplate markup and improve meaningful text ratio.
recommendation.improve_internal_linking: Add internal links to reduce orphan-page risk.
recommendation.fix_html_structure: Fix core HTML structure issues (doctype/head/body/heading hierarchy).
recommendation.fix_hreflang_reciprocal_links: Ensure hreflang links are reciprocal between translated pages.
recommendation.add_hreflang_x_default: Add x-default hreflang for international page sets.
recommendation.verify_bot_access_in_logs: Verify that search bots can access the site and logs are collected.
recommendation.reduce_bot_5xx_errors: Investigate and reduce 5xx responses seen by bots.

newsMediaIntel.title: News / Media Intel
newsMediaIntel.description: Mentions monitoring with topic extraction, sentiment and timeline.

help.label: About block
help.overview: News and media monitoring aggregates mentions from public feeds, then builds topics, timeline and sentiment snapshot.
help.topics: Topics are extracted from titles and snippets with stop-word filtering. They are heuristic indicators, not a linguistic model.
help.timeline: Timeline groups mention counts by publication date to highlight spikes and event windows.
help.mentions: Mentions are deduplicated by URL. Long headlines and links are wrapped to stay inside the card.

descriptions.hash: Generate digest or HMAC to verify integrity and compare artifacts.
descriptions.transform: Convert artifacts between Base64, Hex and URL formats for analysis.
descriptions.ioc: Extract emails, domains, URLs, IPs and Telegram usernames from raw text.
descriptions.classic: Classical and configurable ciphers for quick OSINT experiments and artifact transformation.
descriptions.jwt: Decode JWT header/payload, check timing claims and optionally verify HS signature.

input.label: Input
input.placeholder: Paste text, token, URL or mixed artifact here

help.input: Paste any artifact for quick OSINT transformations: hash, decode, and IOC extraction.
help.result: Result is returned in structured JSON so it is easy to copy to reports or reuse in pivots.

hash.hashMode: Hash mode: use when you need a digest/fingerprint of text for comparison.
hash.hmacMode: HMAC mode: use when you need a keyed signature check (integrity with secret key).

transform.default: Select an operation to convert between common formats.
transform.base64Encode: Useful for packing binary/text into portable ASCII.
transform.base64Decode: Use when artifact looks like Base64 text and you need the original content.
transform.base64UrlEncode: JWT-safe Base64 variant for URLs and tokens.
transform.hexDecode: Decode hexadecimal strings back to text/binary.
transform.urlEncode: Escape text for safe URL query/path usage.
transform.htmlEncode: Escape HTML-sensitive characters to prevent markup interpretation.
transform.htmlDecode: Decode HTML entities back to readable characters.

classic.caesar: Classical shift cipher. Best for simple obfuscation/CTF-style artifacts.
classic.atbash: Mirror substitution cipher. Same operation for encode/decode.
classic.rot13: Fixed shift for latin/cyrillic letters. Often used for light obfuscation.
classic.rot47: ASCII printable rotation. Useful for mixed punctuation payloads.
classic.rot5: Digit-only rotation by +5. Useful for numeric obfuscation.
classic.vigenere: Polyalphabetic cipher with keyword. Supports latin and cyrillic letters in key and text.
classic.railFence: Transposition cipher with zig-zag rails. Configure number of rails.
classic.xor: XOR cipher with key. Encrypt outputs Base64; decrypt expects Base64 input.
classic.affine: Affine substitution cipher for latin letters. A must be coprime with 26.
classic.playfair: Digraph substitution cipher. Works on latin letters; J is merged into I.
classic.columnar: Columnar transposition with a keyword that defines column order.
classic.morse: Morse encode/decode for latin, cyrillic, digits and common punctuation.

ioc.extract: Extracts emails, domains, URLs, IPs and Telegram usernames using pattern matching. Review results manually for false positives.

jwt.inspect: Parses token structure and claims. If secret is set and alg is HS256/384/512, signature verification is attempted.
